title: athlete — model relationships
description: One owned entity: Athlete. Belongs to Tenant (CASCADE) + Branch (RESTRICT) + optionally User (RESTRICT, nullable). Cascades organization + region through branch, application through tenant per hierarchy.md §2. No `application_id` on any row — cascades through `tenant_id → tenants.application_id`. No `organization_id` or `region_id` — cascades through `branch_id → branches.organization_id / branches.region_id`. Every outbound FK stays same-tenant per tenancy-columns.md §5. The `current_age_group_id` is a SNAPSHOT — nightly-recomputed pointer to an AgeGroup row. When the source AgeGroup is deleted, the FK is SET NULL and the next snapshot job re-resolves.

outbound.description: Every Athlete row is tenant-scoped per tenancy-columns.md §3. Cascade delete on TenantErased via `PurgeAthleteDataForErasedTenant` hook — writes compliance audit + migrates athlete_medical_history rows to the compliance-archive table (they outlive the tenant per 7-year regulated-health retention).

Home branch. RESTRICT: branches cannot be hard-deleted while Athletes reference them. Branch archive is blocked by PreventAthleteOrphansOnBranchArchived hook when active athletes exist. Immutable in Wave 3a — transfer flow lands Wave 3b.

OPTIONAL. Null when the athlete has no login (minors typically until 13-16). Non-null when the athlete has an account (adults + older minors). RESTRICT: a User row cannot be soft-deleted or archived while an active Athlete row references it. The archive flow must run first — Athlete.status → archived — which fires AthleteArchived → UnblockUserArchiveWhenLastAthleteArchived lifts the block. IMMUTABLE post-first-link — you cannot repoint an athlete at a different User (that would launder audit trail).

Snapshot — nightly-recomputed pointer to the AgeGroup the athlete currently falls into. SET NULL when the source AgeGroup is soft-deleted OR archived; RollAthleteAgeGroupSnapshotJob's next pass re-resolves against the tenant's current catalog. NOT a hard 'belongs-to' semantically — the athlete belongs to their DOB; the age-group is a computed classifier.

The User who recorded the current consent snapshot (typically the primary AthleteGuardian for minors; the athlete themselves post-adulthood). SET NULL on User archive so the audit trail (via athlete_medical_history + AthleteConsentRecorded event payload) survives while the live reference is nulled.
The User who filed the withdrawal. SET NULL to preserve the athlete row when the withdrawing user's account is later erased.

inbound.description: Downstream modules (Wave 3+) attach to Athlete via the traits shipped here:
examples: athlete-guardian::AthleteGuardian (Wave 3a, sibling) — athlete_id references Athlete.id (CASCADE — when an athlete is hard-deleted, guardian rows go too)
athlete-enrollment::AthleteEnrollment (Wave 3a, sibling) — athlete_id references Athlete.id (RESTRICT — cannot delete an athlete with active/paused enrollments)
teams::TeamMember (Wave 3+) — polymorphic; when member_type='athlete', member_id references Athlete.id
safeguarding::MedicalClearance (Wave 4+) — athlete_id references Athlete.id + reads the medical cluster
inbound.onDelete: RESTRICT by convention on domain FKs referencing athletes.id — Athlete archive cascade-checks live in AthleteObserver.saving. The one CASCADE is AthleteGuardian (sibling module) since a guardian row without its athlete is meaningless.

accessor_chain.description: The Athlete model exposes accessors that keep the PII cluster behind one boundary. The redactor pipeline (from data-classes.json) applies uniformly.

Athlete.consent_summary: { photo_release, medical_disclosure, third_party, recorded_at, recorded_by_user_id, expires_at (12mo from recorded_at when athlete_consent_workflow entitlement is on) }
Athlete.current_age_group: belongsTo (nightly snapshot; not a live join per every-row query)
Athlete.email: user->identity->email WHEN user_id IS NOT NULL, ELSE null
Athlete.phone: user->profile->phone_e164 WHEN user_id IS NOT NULL, ELSE emergency_contact_phone (with permission check)
Athlete.locale: user->profile->locale WHEN user_id IS NOT NULL, ELSE primary_language ?? branch->region->locale
Athlete.timezone: user->profile->timezone WHEN user_id IS NOT NULL, ELSE branch->region->timezone

notes: Athlete carries `application_id` INDIRECTLY via its Tenant (via tenants.application_id) — never as a direct column. Enforced by tenancy-compliance-auditor per tenancy-columns.md §2 (only the 8 named rows carry application_id directly).
Athlete cascades organization + region through its Branch. `athlete.organization_id` and `athlete.region_id` DO NOT EXIST — they are forbidden columns per tenancy-columns.md §5.
Athlete.branch_id is IMMUTABLE post-create in Wave 3a. Wave 3b ships a `athlete_branches` multi-branch pivot + a transfer flow with the AthleteBranchChanged event.

Athlete.user_id is IMMUTABLE POST-FIRST-LINK. You can link once (null → non-null); you cannot re-link to a different user. The correct path for an errantly-linked athlete is: archive the athlete → create a new athlete row + link to the correct user.

Athlete.date_of_birth is IMMUTABLE POST-CREATE. DOB is age-group-eligibility-critical. Typo fixes require super_admin override event (Wave 3+) — Wave 3a has NO OVERRIDE PATH (typos must be handled by archiving + re-creating).

Athlete.current_age_group_id is a SNAPSHOT, not a live join. Every read of `athlete->current_age_group` returns the cached pointer; every enrollment form should use the resolver (AgeGroupResolver::resolveFor(athlete)) for the authoritative computation. The snapshot exists for indexed filtering (list every U12 athlete) and for enrollment-time freezing (see athlete-enrollment::age_group_snapshot_id).

There is NO `Student` / `Learner` / `Trainee` model. Athlete IS the person being coached — the naming reflects the domain-agnostic scope (a swimmer, a basketballer, a music student — all are athletes in the model).
